title: Measurement of the isomeric yield ratio for 88Zr thermal neutron absorption

x4freetext: Measurement of the isomeric yield ratio for 88Zr
thermal neutron absorption

x4freetext: The TRIGA Mark II nuclear research
reactor at the Nuclear Engineering Teaching Lab-
oratory (NETL) at the University of Texas.
The TRIGA Mark II nuclear research reactor at NETL
thermal neutron flux of ~10**12 n/cm2/s. The flux is
derived by scaling an MCNP spectra which has been
tuned by 47 foils and subsequent unfolding with the
SAND-II package with the amount of 99Mo activity
created during exposure of the natural molybdenum
monitoring wire. The flux at 0.025 eV is (4.38+-0.22)
The HPGe system.
A sample of 88Zr was produced at Los Alamos National
Laboratory Isotope Production Facility through proton
irradiation of a yttrium target 89Y(p,2n)88Zr and
was delivered to NETL in 2N HCl solution. As the
sample was over a year past initial separation, the
88Y activity exceeded the 88Zr activity. These
factors motivated a 88Zr/88Y separation. This was
accomplished by immersing the sample within 0.01 M
HDEHP in dodecane. The 88Zr residue was dissolved in
2M nitric acid, transferred dropwise onto Kapton tape,
and evaporated to dryness by heating. Kapton is a
polyimide (C41H22N4O11) film with a silicone adhesive
backing. The dried sample was sealed in two High-
density polyethylene (HDPE) vials for irradiation
along with a natural molybdenum flux monitor wire. A
88Zr sample activity of 5 microCi was chosen to
maximize the number of target atoms without creating an
unmanageable dead-time during post-exposure counting.
The simultaneous measurement of 89mZr and
89Zr requires a high neutron flux as well as prompt
counting after exposure. Zirconium sample,
accompanied by a 6mg natural molybdenum flux monitor,
was irradiated for 10 min. Upon end of irradiation
the sample was counted in a HPGe with an ORTEC DSPEC
50 MCA. Measurement began 180 s after end of
irradiation, at which point the 588 keV 89mZr
activity was 500 counts/s. Spectra were extracted
from the ORTEC MCA, corrected for deadtime, and
background subtracted.

x4freetext: The MINUIT package fitting uncertainties.
BP

x4headers.headerHelp: Dummy incident proj. energy, for broad spectrum

x4freetext: The amounts of 89Zr and 89mZr created by the
88Zr(n,gamma) reaction both depend almost directly on
flux, initial sample size, and cross section. By
taking the ratio of the respective signals from these
products, the uncertainty from these variables is
minimized and the IYR better isolated. Chi-squared
minimization was performed with MINUIT.
